page: Amazon Web Services t3a.2xlarge is a burstable virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The instance is configured with 8 virtual CPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the t3a.2xlarge is positioned in the lower tiers for raw compute and memory throughput. In stress-ng testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 10% category. Similarly, Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores are rated as poor and weak respectively, reflecting the burstable nature of the CPU allocation. Memory bandwidth tests show a weak read-only small block cached speed of 299.02 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write speeds drop to poor levels at 22.36 GB/sec and 11.2 GB/sec. However, the instance demonstrates average performance in Passmark CPU and encryption tests, indicating stable execution for standard cryptographic operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t3a.2xlarge offers a budget-friendly profile for workloads that experience temporary spikes in resource utilization rather than sustained high CPU demands. Due to its burstable CPU allocation model, it is highly cost-effective for non-production environments, development servers, code repositories, and low-traffic web serving. It is also suitable for lightweight database operations, basic background processing, and small-scale application testing where continuous peak performance is not required.
description: Amazon Web Services t3a.2xlarge is a burstable virtual machine instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates that the instance exhibits lower-tier performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling into the poor to weak categories, though it maintains average ratings in Passmark CPU and encryption tests. This resource profile makes the t3a.2xlarge a cost-effective choice for burstable workloads. It is best suited for development environments, low-traffic web serving, code repositories, and light database operations where continuous peak CPU utilization is unnecessary.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t3a.2xlarge is a burstable x86_64 virtual machine instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, designed for budget-friendly and non-sustained workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t3a.2xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU for budget-friendly workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, burstable virtual instance featuring balanced memory and compute resources for non-sustained workloads and development environments.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores
Equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 system memory at a 3 GHz clock speed
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model suitable for variable workloads
Delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps without local storage
Best suited for development servers, code repositories, and low-traffic web applications
